title: Siva Doul
tagline: Siva Doul — spiritual town of Assam

overview.short: Na-Pukhuri Shiva Dol or Rudrasagar Shiva Dol in an 18th-century Hindu temple constructed during the reign of king Lakshmi Singha (1769-1780).
overview.description: Na-Pukhuri Shiva Dol or Rudrasagar Shiva Dol in an 18th-century Hindu temple constructed during the reign of king Lakshmi Singha (1769-1780). This temple is 8 km away from Sivasagar town. This is the last temple built during the Ahom era which represents grandeur before its overall decline in temple building and architectural building. This temple is located on the south bank of Rudrasagar Pukhuri (tank) which was excavated in honor of Rudra Singha.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Ghanashyam House
topPlaces.description: Ghanashyam House or Nati Gosain dol is an early 18th-century brick monument built during the reign of the king Rudra Singha dedicated to the architect Ghanashyam. This monument is situated on the west bank of Joysagar Tank.

topPlaces.name: Rang Ghar
topPlaces.description: The Rang Ghar (Assamese: [ɹɔŋɡʱɔɹ]), (rong ghor meaning "House of Entertainment") is a two-storeyed building which once served as the royal sports-pavilion where Ahom kings and nobles were spectators at games like buffalo fights and other sports at Rupahi Pathar (pathar meaning "field" in Assamese) - particularly during the Rongali Bihu festival in the Ahom capital of Rangpur. It is three kilometres (1.9 mi) away from the center of Sivasagar Town.

topPlaces.name: Rangpur, Assam
topPlaces.description: Sukapha (Tai-Ahom:Tsé-Moon; Assamese: [চুকাফা]) is a City the 4th capital of the Ahom kingdom, was established by Swargadeo Rudra Singha in "Meteka" in 1707 after shifting the capital from Garhgaon. The place holds many monuments build by the Ahom dynasty, the most notable of which are the Talatal Ghar and the Rang Ghar.

topPlaces.name: Namdang Stone Bridge
topPlaces.description: The Namdang Stone Bridge is a historic bridge located a few kilometers away from Sibsagar town in Assam, India. It was constructed in 1703 by craftmen brought from Bengal during the reign of Ahom king Rudra Singha.

topPlaces.name: Talatal Ghar
topPlaces.description: The Talatal Ghar [tɔlatɔl ɡʱɔɹ]) is an 18th-century palace and military base located in Rangpur, 4 kilometres (2.5 mi) from present-day Sivasagar, Assam, India. It is the largest of the monument built during the Ahom era.

topPlaces.name: Chaul Kora, Sivasagar
topPlaces.description: Chaul Kora or Chaulkara is a small rural village in the Sivasagar tehsil, Sivasagar district, Assam state, India. In the census and other sources it is qualified as No.1 in order to distinguish it from the nearly homonymous village of Charul Kora (No.2) in the same district, about 8 km to the northeast.

topPlaces.name: Gourisagar Pukhuri
topPlaces.description: Gourisagar Pukhuri or Gaurisagar Tank is an artificial lake in Sivasagar, Sivasagar district in the state of Assam in India. It is situated 12 km away from Sivasagar.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Dhai Ali
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Dhai Ali was fought between Captain Welsh of East India Company and the Moamorias led by Bharath Singha (the Raja of Rangpur), reinforced by a contingent of troops sent to his aid by the Bengmara chief Sarbananda Singha. It was the last encounter between Captain Welsh and the Moamorias before his recallment.

items.note: Ghanashyam House or Nati Gosain dol is an early 18th-century brick monument built during the reign of the king Rudra Singha dedicated to the architect Ghanashyam. This monument is situated on the west bank of Joysagar Tank.

The Rang Ghar (Assamese: [ɹɔŋɡʱɔɹ]), (rong ghor meaning "House of Entertainment") is a two-storeyed building which once served as the royal sports-pavilion where Ahom kings and nobles were spectators at games like buffalo fights and other sports at Rupahi Pathar (pathar meaning "field" in Assamese) - particularly during the Rongali Bihu festival in the Ahom capital of Rangpur. It is three kilometres (1.9 mi) away from the center of Sivasagar Town.

Sukapha (Tai-Ahom:Tsé-Moon; Assamese: [চুকাফা]) is a City the 4th capital of the Ahom kingdom, was established by Swargadeo Rudra Singha in "Meteka" in 1707 after shifting the capital from Garhgaon. The place holds many monuments build by the Ahom dynasty, the most notable of which are the Talatal Ghar and the Rang Ghar.

The Namdang Stone Bridge is a historic bridge located a few kilometers away from Sibsagar town in Assam, India. It was constructed in 1703 by craftmen brought from Bengal during the reign of Ahom king Rudra Singha.

The Talatal Ghar [tɔlatɔl ɡʱɔɹ]) is an 18th-century palace and military base located in Rangpur, 4 kilometres (2.5 mi) from present-day Sivasagar, Assam, India. It is the largest of the monument built during the Ahom era.

Chaul Kora or Chaulkara is a small rural village in the Sivasagar tehsil, Sivasagar district, Assam state, India. In the census and other sources it is qualified as No.1 in order to distinguish it from the nearly homonymous village of Charul Kora (No.2) in the same district, about 8 km to the northeast.
Gourisagar Pukhuri or Gaurisagar Tank is an artificial lake in Sivasagar, Sivasagar district in the state of Assam in India. It is situated 12 km away from Sivasagar.

The Battle of Dhai Ali was fought between Captain Welsh of East India Company and the Moamorias led by Bharath Singha (the Raja of Rangpur), reinforced by a contingent of troops sent to his aid by the Bengmara chief Sarbananda Singha. It was the last encounter between Captain Welsh and the Moamorias before his recallment.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Siva Doul?
faq.a: The best time to visit Siva Doul is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Siva Doul?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jorhat Airport (48 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (144 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Siva Doul?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Siva Doul at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Siva Doul?
faq.a: Stays in Siva Doul start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Siva Doul?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ghanashyam House, Rang Ghar, Rangpur, Assam, Namdang Stone Bridge, Talatal Ghar.
